title: DeFiRegimeNet — Hybrid Crypto Regime Detection
short_description: Causal per-token HMM regime detection on synthetic crypto panel data with GARCH(1,1)/Student-t DGP, purged combinatorial CV for classifier evaluation, and cross-token Cramér's V association analysis.
long_description: DeFiRegimeNet detects per-token regimes with independent causal Gaussian HMMs, compares logistic regression and XGBoost classifiers against HMM/GMM baselines using CPCV, and evaluates cross-token regime association via Cramér's V. Target labels use GARCH-based volatility forecasting with AST label quarantine.
technical_details:
## Algorithm & Architecture

DeFiRegimeNet implements a four-stage causal regime detection pipeline:

```
CryptoGenerator (GARCH+Student-t DGP)
  → per-token causal HMM (independent, one per token)
  → make_regime_labels (target-date labeling, horizon H=5)
  → CPCV classifier evaluation (logistic, XGBoost)
  → cross-token Cramér's V association matrix
```

## Data Generating Process

- **Generator**: `CryptoGenerator(seed=42, n_years=3, tokens=['BTC','ETH','SOL','AVAX'])`
- **Vol model**: GARCH(1,1) vol clustering per token
- **Innovations**: Student-t fat-tails (df=4)
- **Regime DGP**: Markov 4-state with market_factor_weight=0.70
- **N bars**: 1095 per token (daily, 24/7)

## Causality Guarantees

**CausalRegimeDetector** wraps Gaussian HMM with a causal oracle: label at t depends only on features[:t+1]. Features: ret_lag1, rv_21, mom_21, drawdown — all shift(1) then expanding z-scored (expanding z-score prevents future mean/std leakage).

**Target-date labeling** with GARCH (AST label quarantine): Forward-looking 4-state regime labels at horizon H=5 are constructed via `make_regime_labels`. The forward horizon window is NEVER used as a training feature — the AST scanner quarantines any reference to forward-looking target construction from the feature pipeline.

## Purged Combinatorial CV (CPCV)

- Embargo size = purge size = H = 5 bars
- No train-sample label bleeds into test fold
- HMM/GMM baselines use the full causal sequence WITHOUT CV (asymmetry favors baselines — conservative/honest)

## Model Results

| Model | Accuracy | Log-loss | Notes |
|-------|----------|----------|-------|
| HMM | 0.2595 | 5.1158 | Persistence baseline (no CPCV) |
| GMM | 0.3129 | 4.7474 | Persistence baseline (no CPCV) |
| Logistic | 0.4506 | 1.0280 | CPCV — purged/embargoed |
| XGBoost | 0.4322 | 1.0862 | CPCV — purged/embargoed |

## Cross-Token Cramér's V

Mean off-diagonal V = **0.329** — independently detected per-token sequences show genuine cross-token association. >0.3 indicates shared-factor recovery; <0.15 would indicate independence; 1.0 would indicate a degenerate shared-sequence shortcut.

```
         BTC    ETH    SOL    AVAX
BTC    1.000  0.456  0.253  0.355
ETH    0.456  1.000  0.235  0.382
SOL    0.253  0.235  1.000  0.289
AVAX   0.355  0.382  0.289  1.000
```

30% idiosyncratic noise and label-permutation ambiguity limit recovery of the planted 70% market factor — an honest measure of how hard shared-regime detection is in practice.

## Oracle Test Suite (98 tests)

- `test_causal_hmm_no_lookahead`: Per-token regime at t uses only features[:t+1]
- `test_embargo_purge_geq_horizon`: Both embargo_size and purge_size ≥ H
- `test_ast_label_quarantine`: AST scan confirms no forward-horizon data in feature pipeline
- `test_target_date_labeling`: Labels reference target date t+H, not signal date t
- `test_cramers_v_symmetry`: V[i,j] == V[j,i] and diagonal == 1.0
- `test_baseline_asymmetry_direction`: HMM log-loss > logistic log-loss (baselines disadvantaged by no-CV is honest)
